heading: Under Secretary of Agriculture for Marketing and Regulatory Programs
label: 7005 Under Secretary of Agriculture for Marketing and Regulatory Programs
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of , as added by . Subsec. (e) of amended , Government Organization and Employees.section 285 of Pub. L. 103–354Pub. L. 105–277section 285 of Pub. L. 103–354section 5314 of Title 5
amendments: CodificationSection is comprised of , as added by . Subsec. (e) of amended , Government Organization and Employees.section 285 of Pub. L. 103–354Pub. L. 105–277section 285 of Pub. L. 103–354section 5314 of Title 5

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: a Authorization
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to establish in the Department the position of Under Secretary of Agriculture for Marketing and Regulatory Programs.

children.heading: Confirmation required
children.label: b Confirmation required
children.text: If the Secretary establishes the position of Under Secretary of Agriculture for Marketing and Regulatory Programs authorized under subsection (a), the Under Secretary shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.heading: Principal functions
children.label: 1 Principal functions
children.text: Upon establishment, the Secretary shall delegate to the Under Secretary of Agriculture for Marketing and Regulatory Programs those functions and duties under the jurisdiction of the Department that are related to agricultural marketing, animal and plant health inspection, grain inspection, and packers and stockyards.

children.heading: Additional functions
children.label: 2 Additional functions
children.text: The Under Secretary of Agriculture for Marketing and Regulatory Programs shall perform such other functions and duties as may be required by law or prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Succession
children.label: d Succession
children.text: Any official who is serving as Assistant Secretary of Agriculture for Marketing and Regulatory Programs on , and who was appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, shall not be required to be reappointed under subsection (b) to the successor position authorized under subsection (a) if the Secretary establishes the position, and the official occupies the new position, within 180 days after (or such later date set by the Secretary if litigation delays rapid succession).October 21, 1998October 21, 1998
